intro: Therogeron leptophyllus, also known as Wild Ergot, is a flowering plant species in the bryophyte family, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and unique habit of producing seeds in clusters. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, antiviral, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Therogeron leptophyllus include alkaloids, such as ergine and erginine, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Therogeron leptophyllus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
